name: 121.5 guard
note: 121.5 MHz — the international aeronautical emergency (VHF 'guard') frequency, monitored by ATC and aircraft worldwide.

name: 2 m calling
note: 146.52 MHz — the 2 m national FM simplex calling frequency: where hams meet before moving off-channel.

name: 6 m calling
note: 52.525 MHz — the 6 m 'Magic Band' national FM simplex calling frequency, the first place to listen when a sporadic-E opening lights the band up.

name: 1.25 m calling
note: 223.5 MHz — the 1.25 m (222 MHz) national FM simplex calling frequency.

name: 70 cm calling
note: 446.0 MHz — the 70 cm national FM simplex calling frequency, where you call before dropping to a simplex working channel.

name: 243.0 guard
note: 243.0 MHz — the military UHF emergency ('guard') frequency, the NATO counterpart to civil 121.5.
